titleStmt.title: SII 24.75: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswamy
No. 75.

summary.p: The inscription is in Kannaḍa. It is damaged and worn out in places and records a provision made for burning two lamps in the temple of god Śrīraṅga by the Kannaḍasandhivigrahi and Daṇḍanāyaka (name lost) of king Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI) in the 29th year (A.D. 1099-1100) of the reign of Kulōttuṅgadēva (Kulōttuṅga I). Gives the names of seven Kōns such as Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-kōn, Karuṇākara-kōn, Śrī-Narasiṅga-kōn, Tirumāliru[ñ]jōlai-kōn, etc., who were enjoined to supply the required quantity of ghee for burning the lamps. The record seems also to refer to the plastering, with lime mortar, caused to be done to the shrine of Sēnāpati (Vishvaksēna) probably by the same donor.
Published in Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XXXVI, pp. 203 ff. and plate.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
mahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśraya kuḷatiḷakaṁ
bhuvanamallabhaṭṭāraṟa kannaḍasandhi vigrahi daṇḍanāyakaṁ
kulōttuṁgadēvarge yāṇḍu Ippattoṁbhattāgalu
kṣētrada śrīraṁgadēvara veḷekeraḍakkaṁ koṭṭa
ya kōn I [Aḷa]geyamanavāḷakōn . tiru
n . karuṇāgara kōn . Aḷasiṁgakōn . tirumāri jōlu
Āṁtēṇcumānasada vasada loppisida[r .] Avaru divaseṁ
ppavaṁ dēvara veḷakeṁge taṁdeṟivar Ācaṁdrākka[¨r*]sthāyiyāgi
Idha¨rmmayaṁ śrīraṁgadēvara śrī vaiṣṇavara rakṣe .
va sēnāpatiya kogeleyaṁ suṇṇada sōte
dar . �
Digital edition of SII 24.75 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
